description: Fraud involving Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions through deceptive collect requests, fake refunds, malicious payment links, QR-code traps, impersonation, or other social engineering techniques used to steal money or payment credentials.
recommended_actions: Immediately contact your bank or payment app support.
Call 1930 without delay if money was transferred.
Report the incident on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal.
Do not approve unknown collect requests or scan QR codes to receive money.
Preserve transaction receipts, screenshots, URLs, UPI IDs and chat logs.
prevention_tips: Verify the recipient before making payments.
Never share your UPI PIN, OTP or card details.
Read the payment screen carefully before confirming.
Keep your UPI app updated and enable transaction alerts.

rules.name: Collect Request Scam
rules.explanation: Victim is asked to approve a UPI collect or payment request, often by claiming it is needed to receive money.
examples: Approve the collect request to receive your refund.
Accept the payment request to claim your prize.

rules.name: UPI PIN Request
rules.explanation: Attempts to obtain the victim's UPI PIN or a similar payment authorization code.
examples: Enter your UPI PIN to receive money.
Share the PIN to complete the payment.

rules.name: Refund or Cashback Scam
rules.explanation: Uses refunds, cashback or rewards to trick victims into approving a request, scanning a QR code or entering sensitive details.
examples: Claim your refund by approving this request.
Cashback is pending. Enter the PIN to receive it.

rules.name: Payment Link Scam
rules.explanation: Fraud through malicious payment links that redirect victims to phishing or payment-approval pages.
examples: Click this payment link to receive Rs 5000.
Use the UPI link to complete your refund claim.

rules.name: Fake Customer Support
rules.explanation: Fraudster impersonates official support and pressures the victim to approve a request, share credentials or install a malicious app.
examples: I am calling from your bank's UPI support team.
Customer care needs your PIN to fix the issue.

rules.name: QR Code Receive Money Trap
rules.explanation: Victim is told to scan a QR code to receive money, but the scan actually authorizes a payment or collect request.
examples: Scan this QR to receive Rs 5000.
Scan the code to get your refund.

rules.name: Merchant Buyer Seller Scam
rules.explanation: Targets buyers and sellers on online marketplaces with fake payment confirmations, refund tricks or QR-based traps.
examples: I'm sending payment through QR.
Approve the request so I can release your booking amount.

rules.name: Account KYC or Suspension Threat
rules.explanation: Uses KYC, account blocking or wallet suspension threats to force the victim into quick action.
examples: Your UPI account will be blocked unless you verify now.
KYC pending. Update immediately to avoid suspension.

rules.name: Malicious App or Screen Share Request
rules.explanation: Requests installation of a remote access or malicious app that can capture payment approvals or device data.
examples: Install this APK to complete UPI verification.
Share your screen so I can fix the payment issue.

rules.name: Urgency and Limited Time Pressure
rules.explanation: Uses urgency to pressure the victim into approving a request or sending money without verification.
examples: Act now! Approve the request within 5 minutes.
The offer expires today. Send the UPI payment now.
